segments.text: Genetic Manipulation of the Mouse

Mouse Genetic Engineering
1)  Dominant expression
2)  Knockouts-recessive, must be homozygous
3)  Tissue-specific knockouts or knock-ins

Tissue-Specific Knockout Technology
Why make tissue specific knockouts?
Many genes will cause death if knocked out in all tissues.  Tissue specific knockouts will allow you address the function of a given gene, in a given tissue.
How can you knock out a gene in a specific tissue?
Flank the gene with signals that allow it to be cut out, when a tissue-specific “scissors” are expressed

Tissue-Specific Knockout Technology
Cre/lox
Phage P1 enzyme Cre recombinase catalyzes recombination between 34 bp loxP recognition sequences.
The P1 genome is ~93kb and contains at least 117 genes

Infective virions of P1 contain linear, double stranded,  cyclically permuted, with a terminal redundancy of 10-15 kb.
After infection, the viral DNA circularizes by recombination between redundant sequences to enter a lytic or lysogenic path

Flox
To flank a gene of interest with loxP recognition sequences so that it can be cut out.

Tissue specific knockout:	Step 1
Construct mouse expressing cre-recombinase in the desired tissue

Tissue specific knockout:	Step 2
Construct a mouse that has the tissue specific gene of interest floxed

Tissue specific knockout:	Step 3
Mate the mouse with the tissue specific cre-recombinase to the mouse with the floxed gene of interest
Tissue specific knockout:	Results
Tissue specific knockout:	Results
One of many examples of tissue-specific knock-outs

Tissue specific “knock in”
Stop is floxed

Die
Appear Normal

Pax3-transcription factor
Forkhead-transcription factor
The t(2;13)- mediated Pax3:Fkhr fusion gene is believed to play a key role in 55%–75% of alveolar rhabdomyosarcomas
The t(1;13)-mediated Pax7:Fkhr fusion gene is believed to play a key role in 10%–22% of alveolar rhabdomyosarcomas
Both of these chimeric genes results in production of a novel transcription factor that has the DNA-binding domain from Pax3
or Pax7, important regulators of muscle development, fused to the potent transactivation domain of Fkhr.

Mouse Genetic Engineering
CRISPR
-Can be used in any organism, not just mice
Humanized mice

Major points:
Gene editing technology (any organism)
Derived from bacterial adaptive immune system
Invading DNA is inserted into bacterial genome
Transcripts made from inserted DNA act as guides to mark invading DNA by hybridization, followed by degradation with Cas protein

CRISPR/Cas9
Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats
CRISPR associated Protein
Streptococcus pyogenes
Sreptococcus thermophilus

Virus or plasmid DNA “Spacers”
Bacterial DNA direct “Repeats”

Guide RNA and Cas9 protein direct cleavage
(Guide RNA)
(Cas9 enzyme cuts)

Generation of Mouse “Landing Pad”
